776	/-! ### L2 `q_*`-coherence chain: push–pull turns a coproduct into a product
777	
778	The binary disjoint-cover decomposition `isIso_coprodDecompMap` is upgraded to a statement about
779	the push–pull object `pushPullObj F (Over.mk q)` (`q = coprod.desc pA pB`).  The single substantive
780	node is the per-leg coherence `pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★): the contravariant push–pull map of
781	the over-inclusion `Over.homMk c : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q` is, up to the canonical leg iso
782	`pushPullCoprodLegIso`, the pushforward of the disjoint-cover restriction unit.  This lets the
783	canonical comparison `prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)` be matched against
784	the manifestly-iso chain through `coprodDecompMap`. -/
785	
786	/-- The canonical leg iso identifying `q_*(c_*(M.restrict c))` (a factor of `q_*(P ⨯ Q)`, where
787	`M = q^* F` and `c` is a coproduct inclusion `coprod.inl`/`coprod.inr`) with the push–pull object
788	`pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC)` along the over-triangle `wC : c ≫ q = pC`.  Project-local component of
789	the L2 `q_*`-coherence chain. -/
790	private noncomputable def pushPullCoprodLegIso {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
791	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
792	    (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward c).obj
793	        (((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F).restrict c)) ≅
794	      pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC) :=
795	  (pushforward q).mapIso ((pushforward c).mapIso
796	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≪≫
797	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp c q).app F ≪≫
798	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr wC).app F)) ≪≫
799	  eqToIso (congrArg (fun p => (pushforward p).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback pC).obj F)) wC)
800	
801	-- The final `congr 1` discharges the proof-irrelevant `eqToHom` over-triangle transports against
802	-- concrete pushforward/pullback objects, whose `whnf` exceeds the default heartbeat budget.
803	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
804	/-- (★) Per-leg coherence: the push–pull map of the over-inclusion `Over.homMk c : Over.mk pC ⟶
805	Over.mk q` is, through the canonical leg iso, the pushforward of the disjoint-cover restriction unit
806	`(restrictAdjunction c).unit`.  This is the bridge that converts the canonical comparison map
807	`prod.lift (pushPullMap F …)` into the manifestly-iso `coprodDecompMap` chain.  Project-local. -/
808	private lemma pushPull_binary_leg_coherence {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
809	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
810	    pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q) =
811	      (pushforward q).map
812	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) ≫
813	        (pushPullCoprodLegIso q c pC wC F).hom := by
814	  have hraw : pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q)
815	      = rawPushPullMap c q pC wC F := rfl
816	  rw [hraw, rawPushPullMap_self_gen]
817	  have hLAU : (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≫
818	        (pushforward c).map
819	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).hom.app
820	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) =
821	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction c).unit.app
822	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) :=
823	    Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app _ _ _
824	  subst wC
825	  simp only [pushPullCoprodLegIso, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, eqToIso.hom,
826	    Iso.app_hom, Category.comp_id,
827	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr, eqToIso_refl, Iso.refl_hom, NatTrans.id_app]
828	  rw [← hLAU]
829	  simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]; rfl
830	
831	end BinaryDecomp
832	
833	/-- Push–pull on a binary coproduct of two legs is the binary product of the two leg push–pulls.
834	The forward map is the canonical `prod.lift` of the two push–pull maps of the coproduct inclusions
835	(the mandatory framing the downstream section-identification needs); it is shown to be an
836	isomorphism by matching it leg-by-leg, via the per-leg coherence
837	`pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★), against the manifestly-invertible reference chain through the
838	binary disjoint-union decomposition `coprodDecompMap`.  Project-local L2 assembly
839	(blueprint `lem:pushPull_binary_coprod_prod`). -/
840	noncomputable def pushPull_binary_coprod_prod (F : X.Modules) (Y₀ Y₁ : Over X) :
841	    pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom)) ≅
842	      pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ := by
843	  set q : Y₀.left ⨿ Y₁.left ⟶ X := Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom with hq
844	  set M := (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F with hM
845	  have wInl : (Limits.coprod.inl : Y₀.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₀.hom := Limits.coprod.inl_desc _ _
846	  have wInr : (Limits.coprod.inr : Y₁.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₁.hom := Limits.coprod.inr_desc _ _
847	  set overInl : Y₀ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inl wInl with hoverInl
848	  set overInr : Y₁ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inr wInr with hoverInr
849	  haveI : IsIso (coprodDecompMap M) := isIso_coprodDecompMap M
850	  -- The per-leg identifications.  Their codomains are pinned to `pushPullObj F Y₀`/`Y₁`
851	  -- (defeq to the `pushPullObj F (Over.mk Y₀.hom)` produced by `pushPullCoprodLegIso`); the
852	  -- syntactic pin is essential so the `Category.assoc`/`prod.map_fst` rewrites below can match
853	  -- the trailing `prod.fst` on `pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁`.
854	  set idiso₀ : (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward Limits.coprod.inl).obj (M.restrict Limits.coprod.inl))
855	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ :=
856	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F with hidiso0
857	  set idiso₁ : (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward Limits.coprod.inr).obj (M.restrict Limits.coprod.inr))
858	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
859	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F with hidiso1
860	  have hcoh0 : pushPullMap F overInl
861	      = (pushforward q).map ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction Limits.coprod.inl).unit.app M)
862	          ≫ idiso₀.hom := by
863	    rw [hidiso0]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F
864	  have hcoh1 : pushPullMap F overInr
865	      = (pushforward q).map ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction Limits.coprod.inr).unit.app M)
866	          ≫ idiso₁.hom := by
867	    rw [hidiso1]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F
868	  set chainIso : (pushforward q).obj M ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
869	    (pushforward q).mapIso (asIso (coprodDecompMap M)) ≪≫
870	      Limits.PreservesLimitPair.iso (pushforward q) _ _ ≪≫
871	      Limits.prod.mapIso idiso₀ idiso₁ with hchain
872	  -- Match the canonical comparison against the reference chain entirely through `prod.lift`
873	  -- identities (`prod.lift_map`, `prod.comp_lift`, and `prodComparison = prod.lift (q_* fst)
874	  -- (q_* snd)`), avoiding any `≫ prod.fst` projection that the surrounding pushforward objects
875	  -- make awkward to reassociate.
876	  have hcmp : Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr) = chainIso.hom := by
877	    rw [hcoh0, hcoh1, hchain, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom,
878	      Limits.prod.mapIso_hom, Limits.PreservesLimitPair.iso_hom]
879	    show Limits.prod.lift _ _ =
880	      (pushforward q).map (coprodDecompMap M) ≫
881	        Limits.prod.lift ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.fst) ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.snd)
882	          ≫ Limits.prod.map idiso₀.hom idiso₁.hom
883	    rw [Limits.prod.lift_map, Limits.prod.comp_lift, ← Functor.map_comp_assoc,
884	      ← Functor.map_comp_assoc, coprodDecompMap, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Limits.prod.lift_snd]
885	    rfl
886	  haveI : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)) := by
887	    rw [hcmp]; infer_instance
888	  exact asIso (Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr))
889	
890	/-- The forward map of `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod` in its canonical `prod.lift`-of-push–pull-maps
891	form (the binary case of the canonical framing kept through the finite induction). -/
892	private lemma pushPull_binary_coprod_prod_hom (F : X.Modules) (Y₀ Y₁ : Over X) :
893	    (pushPull_binary_coprod_prod F Y₀ Y₁).hom =
894	      Limits.prod.lift
895	        (pushPullMap F (Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inl (Limits.coprod.inl_desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom) :
896	          Y₀ ⟶ Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom)))
897	        (pushPullMap F (Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inr (Limits.coprod.inr_desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom) :
898	          Y₁ ⟶ Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom))) := rfl
899	
900	/-! ### Finite-index induction: push–pull turns a finite coproduct into a finite product
901	
902	The binary `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod` is upgraded to an arbitrary finite index `ι` by
903	`Finite.induction_empty_option`.  The chain runs through four leaves:
904	* `pushPullObjCongr` — transport a push–pull object along a slice iso;
905	* `overSigmaOptionIso` — slice lift of the `Option`-coproduct split;
906	* `piOptionIso` — the dual `Option`-product split;
907	* `pushPull_coprod_prod_empty` — the empty base case.
908	-/
909	
910	/-- Push–pull objects transport along a slice isomorphism `e : Y ≅ Y'` in `Over X`.  The
911	push–pull object is a contravariant functor of its slice argument, so the forward map is
912	`pushPullMap F e.inv` and the backward map `pushPullMap F e.hom`.  Project-local
913	(blueprint `lem:pushPullObjCongr`). -/
914	noncomputable def pushPullObjCongr (F : X.Modules) {Y Y' : Over X} (e : Y ≅ Y') :
915	    pushPullObj F Y ≅ pushPullObj F Y' where
916	  hom := pushPullMap F e.inv
917	  inv := pushPullMap F e.hom
918	  hom_inv_id := by rw [← pushPullMap_comp, e.hom_inv_id, pushPullMap_id]
919	  inv_hom_id := by rw [← pushPullMap_comp, e.inv_hom_id, pushPullMap_id]
920	
921	/-- Slice lift of the `Option`-coproduct split (`sigmaOptionIso`): for a family of slice
922	objects `legs : Option α → Over X`, the coproduct slice object `Over.mk (Sigma.desc (·.hom))`
923	is isomorphic to the binary-split slice object.  Built as `Over.isoMk` of the underlying
924	`sigmaOptionIso` together with the structure-map compatibility.  Project-local
925	(blueprint `lem:over_sigmaOptionIso`). -/
926	noncomputable def overSigmaOptionIso {α : Type*} (legs : Option α → Over X)
927	    [HasCoproduct (fun o => (legs o).left)]
928	    [HasCoproduct (fun a : α => (legs (some a)).left)]
929	    [HasBinaryCoproduct (legs none).left (∐ fun a : α => (legs (some a)).left)] :
930	    Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun o => (legs o).hom)) ≅
931	      Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc (legs none).hom
932	        (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun a : α => (legs (some a)).hom))) :=
933	  Over.isoMk (sigmaOptionIso (fun o => (legs o).left)) (by
934	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun o => ?_)
935	    rcases o with _ | a
936	    · simp only [sigmaOptionIso, Over.mk_left, Over.mk_hom,
937	        Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inl_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
938	    · simp only [sigmaOptionIso, Over.mk_left, Over.mk_hom, Category.assoc,
939	        Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inr_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])
940	
941	/-- The dual `Option`-product split: for `W : Option α → C` the total product splits off its
942	`none` factor.  Project-local (blueprint `lem:piOptionIso`). -/
943	noncomputable def piOptionIso {C : Type*} [Category C] {α : Type*} (W : Option α → C)
944	    [HasProduct W] [HasProduct (fun a : α => W (some a))]
945	    [HasBinaryProduct (W none) (∏ᶜ fun a : α => W (some a))] :
946	    (∏ᶜ W) ≅ W none ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun a : α => W (some a)) where
947	  hom := Limits.prod.lift (Limits.Pi.π W none)
948	    (Limits.Pi.lift (fun a => Limits.Pi.π W (some a)))
949	  inv := Limits.Pi.lift (fun o => Option.rec Limits.prod.fst
950	    (fun a => Limits.prod.snd ≫ Limits.Pi.π (fun a : α => W (some a)) a) o)
951	  hom_inv_id := by
952	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
953	    rintro (_ | a)
954	    · simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Category.id_comp]
955	    · rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, ← Category.assoc,
956	        Limits.prod.lift_snd, Limits.Pi.lift_π]
957	  inv_hom_id := by
958	    apply Limits.prod.hom_ext
959	    · simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp]
960	    · apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
961	      intro a
962	      simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.prod.lift_snd, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp]
963	
964	/-- The coproduct inclusion of leg `i`, viewed as an over-morphism into the descent object
965	`Over.mk (Sigma.desc (·.hom))`. -/
966	noncomputable def coprodOverIncl {ι : Type*} (legs : ι → Over X)
967	    [HasCoproduct (fun i => (legs i).left)] (i : ι) :
968	    legs i ⟶ Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (legs i).hom)) :=
969	  Over.homMk (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun i => (legs i).left) i) (by simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])
970	
971	/-- The canonical comparison map from the push–pull object on the coproduct of the legs to
972	the product of the per-leg push–pull objects: the `Pi.lift` of the push–pull maps of the
973	coproduct inclusions.  This is the canonical framing kept throughout the finite induction. -/
974	noncomputable def coprodToProdMap {ι : Type*} (F : X.Modules) (legs : ι → Over X)
975	    [HasCoproduct (fun i => (legs i).left)]
976	    [HasProduct (fun i => pushPullObj F (legs i))] :
977	    pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (legs i).hom))) ⟶
978	      ∏ᶜ fun i => pushPullObj F (legs i) :=
979	  Limits.Pi.lift (fun i => pushPullMap F (coprodOverIncl legs i))
980	
981	/-- A sheaf of modules over an empty (initial) scheme is a zero object.  All its sections
982	vanish: the structure sheaf has subsingleton sections over every open (`IsEmpty ↥Z`), so every
983	module of sections is subsingleton, and the underlying abelian presheaf is pointwise zero.  We
984	reflect `𝟙 = 0` through the faithful `toPresheaf`. -/
985	private lemma isZero_modules_of_isEmpty {Z : Scheme} [IsEmpty ↥Z] (M : Z.Modules) :
986	    IsZero M := by
987	  rw [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero]
988	  apply (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf Z).map_injective
989	  rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf Z).map_zero]
990	  ext U x
991	  haveI : Subsingleton ↑(((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf Z).obj M).obj (op U)) := by
992	    have h : Subsingleton ↑Γ(M, U) := Module.subsingleton ↑Γ(Z, U) _
993	    exact h
994	  exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
995	
996	/-- Empty base case of the finite induction: for the empty index the comparison map
997	`coprodToProdMap` is an isomorphism (both source and target are terminal — the push–pull
998	of the initial scheme and the empty product).  Project-local
999	(blueprint `lem:pushPull_coprod_prod_empty`). -/
1000	private theorem pushPull_coprod_prod_empty (F : X.Modules) (legs : PEmpty.{u + 1} → Over X) :
1001	    IsIso (coprodToProdMap F legs) := by
1002	  -- Target: the empty product is terminal.
1003	  have hY : Limits.IsTerminal (∏ᶜ fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => pushPullObj F (legs i)) :=
1004	    Limits.IsTerminal.ofUniqueHom (fun _ => Limits.Pi.lift (fun i => i.elim))
1005	      (fun _ m => Limits.Pi.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => i.elim))
1006	  -- Source: push–pull of the initial scheme.  `pushforward q` is a right adjoint, so it
1007	  -- preserves the terminal object; and `(pullback q).obj F` is terminal over the initial
1008	  -- scheme (its structure sheaf has zero sections, so every module sheaf is terminal).
1009	  have hX : Limits.IsTerminal (pushPullObj F
1010	      (Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => (legs i).hom)))) := by
1011	    -- `pushPullObj F Y = (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((pullback Y.hom).obj F)`; pushforward preserves
1012	    -- zero objects (it is additive), and the pulled-back module lives over the initial scheme
1013	    -- `∐ PEmpty`, whose only sheaf of modules is zero.
1014	    refine (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_isZero (Scheme.Modules.pushforward _) ?_).isTerminal
1015	    -- The base scheme `∐ PEmpty` is the initial scheme, hence has empty carrier; every sheaf of
1016	    -- modules over it is a zero object.
1017	    haveI : IsEmpty ↥((Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => (legs i).hom)).left) := by
1018	      rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.isInitial_iff_isEmpty]
1019	      exact ⟨Limits.isColimitEquivIsInitialOfIsEmpty Scheme _
1020	        (Limits.colimit.isColimit (Discrete.functor (fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => (legs i).left)))⟩
1021	    exact isZero_modules_of_isEmpty _
1022	  exact Limits.isIso_of_isTerminal hX hY _
1023	
1024	/-- Reindexing step of the finite induction: the comparison map's iso-status transports
1025	along an equivalence `e : α ≃ β` of index types.  Project-local. -/
1026	private theorem coprodToProd_isIso_of_equiv (F : X.Modules) {α β : Type u} (e : α ≃ β)

1623	  -- which is what makes `hcompat` close (the canonical `sectionCechAugV` is exactly the evaluated
1624	  -- Čech augmentation transported across `coreIso_objIso 0`).
1625	  let coreIso : (GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj
1626	        (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1627	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj
1628	            (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F)) ≅ sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V :=
1629	    HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun p => coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F p V)
1630	      (coreIso_comm 𝒰 F V)
1631	  -- (adapter) The augmentation node `GV(Ψ F)` is the section group `Γ(V, F)`: definitional,
1632	  -- since `restrictScalars (𝟙 ·)` and `toPresheaf` leave the underlying abelian-group presheaf
1633	  -- unchanged and evaluation extracts the section over `V`.
1634	  let eY : GV.obj ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1635	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).obj F) ≅
1636	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V) := Iso.refl _
1637	  -- (compat) The evaluated Čech augmentation equals the canonical `sectionCechAugV` read through
1638	  -- `coreIso_objIso 0`; definitional, since `sectionCechAugV` is by construction
1639	  -- `GV(Ψ(cechAugmentation)) ≫ (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom` and `eY.hom = 𝟙`.
1640	  have hcompat : GV.map ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1641	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)) ≫
1642	        (HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoApp coreIso 0).hom = eY.hom ≫ sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V := by
1643	    have happ : (HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoApp coreIso 0).hom = (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom :=
1644	      congrArg Iso.hom (HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents_app _ _ 0)
1645	    rw [happ, sectionCechAugV]
1646	    exact (Category.id_comp _).symm
1647	  -- Peel the augmentation node off `D` with `mapHC_augment_iso` (twice), then glue the
1648	  -- non-augmented `coreIso` to the augmentation data with `augmentCochainIso`.
1649	  exact (GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).mapIso
1650	      (mapHC_augment_iso (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1651	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) hΨadd (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)
1652	        (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)) ≪≫
1653	    mapHC_augment_iso GV ‹GV.Additive› (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1654	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F))
1655	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1656	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F))
1657	      (map_augment_cond (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1658	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) hΨadd (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)
1659	        (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)) ≪≫
1660	    augmentCochainIso coreIso eY (GV.map ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1661	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)))
1662	      (map_augment_cond GV ‹GV.Additive› (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1663	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F))
1664	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1665	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F))
1666	        (map_augment_cond (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1667	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) hΨadd (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)
1668	          (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F))) (sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V)
1669	      (sectionCechAugV_comp_d 𝒰 F V) hcompat
1670	
1671	/-! ## Stub 6 — Contracting homotopy on the augmented concrete section Čech complex -/
1672	
1673	/- Planner strategy:
1674	Goal: `Homotopy (𝟙 ((sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).augment ε hε)) 0`
1675	assuming `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix`, where
1676	  `sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V = sectionCechComplex (fun i : 𝒰.I₀ => coverOpen 𝒰 i ⊓ V) Fp`
1677	is the non-augmented complex and `ε`, `hε` are the augmentation data.
1678	
1679	This is PURELY COMBINATORIAL — no affine vanishing, no qcoh, no tilde.
1680	
1681	Route:
1682	
1683	(A) IDENTIFY THE FAMILY: `U'_σ := coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V = ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)`.
1684	    `D'` is the alternating coface complex of the cosimplicial object
1685	    `sectionCechCosimplicial (fun i => coverOpen 𝒰 i ⊓ V) Fp`.
1686	
1687	(B) MAXIMUM PROPERTY: Since `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix`, we have
1688	    `coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix ⊓ V = V`. Therefore `U'_{i_fix..σ} = U'_σ` for any `σ`
1689	    (prepending `i_fix` does not shrink the open). Equivalently, the prepend map
1690	    `σ ↦ Fin.cons i_fix σ` is the IDENTITY at the coefficient level:
1691	    for each multi-index `σ : Fin m → 𝒰.I₀`:
1692	      `⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (Fin.cons i_fix σ k) ⊓ V) = ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)`.
1693	    This is because the `k=0` factor is `coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix ⊓ V = V`, which is ≥ every
1694	    other factor (since `U'_j = coverOpen 𝒰 j ⊓ V ≤ V`); hence the iInf is unchanged.
1695	
1696	(C) INSTANTIATE THE DEPENDENT ENGINE: Set
1697	    `A m σ := Fp.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)))`
1698	    `δ m σ j := F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf ...)).op`  (face restriction)
1699	    `c m σ := 𝟙` (or the identity map via the equality from (B))
1700	    Then the Dependent engine hypotheses hold:
1701	    * `hu`: unit identity `c ∘ δ₀ = id` — follows from (B) (prepending `i_fix` at position 0
1702	      recovers the same open, so the restriction is the identity).
1703	    * `hsh`: shift identity `c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c` — follows from `cons_comp_succAbove_succ`.
1704	    Call `CombinatorialCech.depHomotopy i_fix δ c` to get the homotopy maps, and
1705	    `CombinatorialCech.depHomotopy_spec` to obtain `d∘h + h∘d = id`.
1706	
1707	(D) PACKAGE: Wrap the pointwise identity `depHomotopy_spec` as a `Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0` using
1708	    `CochainComplex.homotopyOfEq` or by constructing the `Homotopy` directly from the maps.
1709	
1710	Key Lean names:
1711	- `CombinatorialCech.depDiff` (CechAcyclic.lean, namespace `CombinatorialCech`)
1712	- `CombinatorialCech.depHomotopy`
1713	- `CombinatorialCech.depHomotopy_spec`
1714	- `sectionCechCosimplicial`, `sectionCechComplex` (PresheafCech.lean)
1715	- `le_coverInterOpen_iff` (FreePresheafComplex.lean:729)
1716	
1717	NOTE: The `\uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}` edge in the blueprint is ONLY the Lean home of
1718	the `Dependent` engine — NOT a math dependency. Invoke no affine vanishing.
1719	
1720	Difficulty: MEDIUM (combinatorial assembly; the Dependent engine does the heavy lifting). -/
1721	noncomputable def cechSection_contractible (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1722	    (F : X.Modules) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1723	    (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix) :
1724	    Homotopy (𝟙 ((sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).augment (sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V)
1725	      (sectionCechAugV_comp_d 𝒰 F V))) 0 :=
1726	  -- Stub 6 (the prepend-`i_fix` contracting homotopy on the AUGMENTED concrete section complex
1727	  -- `D'_aug = (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).augment (sectionCechAugV …) …`, with shared canonical
1728	  -- augmentation so the consumer glue `isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero` matches the
1729	  -- `D'` of `cechSection_complex_iso`).  Positive Čech degrees: `CombinatorialCech.depHomotopy`/
1730	  -- `depHomotopy_spec` (prepend `i_fix`; identity on coefficients since `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix`
1731	  -- ⟹ `coverInterOpen (i_fix :: σ) ⊓ V = coverInterOpen σ ⊓ V`).  Degree-0 augmentation node: the
1732	  -- explicit `π_{i_fix}` section-equalizer identity (the `dep*` engine covers only degrees ≥1).
1733	  -- Wraps the pointwise identity into `Homotopy (𝟙 D'_aug) 0` (`sectionCechProductEquiv` to move
1734	  -- between the `Ab`-valued `∏ᶜ` complex and the dependent functions the engine consumes).
1735	  sorry

8781	\begin{lemma}
8782	[Per-leg naturality of the core comparison coface]
8783	  \label{lem:coreIso_comm_leg}
8784	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coreIso_comm_leg}
8785	  \uses{lem:coreIso_obj_iso, lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:pushPull_sigma_iso,
8786	    lem:pushPull_leg_sections, lem:section_cech_product_equiv,
8787	    lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}
8788	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso}, fix a degree \(p\), a coface index
8789	  \(k \le p+1\), and a multi-index \(\sigma' : \mathrm{Fin}(p+2) \to I\). Read the degree-\(p\)
8790	  and degree-\((p+1)\) terms of both complexes through the product equivalence
8791	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}). Then
8792	  the \(\sigma'\)-coordinate of the evaluated push--pull coface followed by the object iso,
8793	  \(G_V\bigl(\Psi(\delta^{\mathrm{nerve}}_k)\bigr) \cdot (\mathrm{objIso}\,(p+1)).\mathrm{hom}\),
8794	  equals the presheaf face restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma', k)\) — the
8795	  value of \(\mathcal{F}\) on the inclusion of intersection opens
8796	  \[
8797	    \bigcap_{j}\bigl(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma' j) \cap V\bigr)
8798	      \;\subseteq\;
8799	    \bigcap_{l}\bigl(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,((\sigma' \circ d_k) l) \cap V\bigr)
8800	  \]
8801	  — applied to the \((\sigma' \circ d_k)\)-coordinate of the image of the source under
8802	  \((\mathrm{objIso}\,p).\mathrm{hom}\). In words: the projection of the evaluated push--pull
8803	  coface onto the \(\sigma'\)-component is exactly the \(\mathcal{F}\)-restriction along the
8804	  \(k\)-th face inclusion.
8805	\end{lemma}
8806	\begin{proof}
8807	  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:pushPull_sigma_iso, lem:pushPull_leg_sections,
8808	    lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}
8809	  Unwind \((\mathrm{objIso}\,(p+1)).\mathrm{hom}\) through its construction in
8810	  Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso}: it is the evaluated push--pull product iso
8811	  \(\operatorname{pushPull\_eval\_prod\_iso}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso}),
8812	  reindexed factorwise by the open-meet identity (Lemma~\ref{lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}).
8813	  The product iso factors through the coproduct-to-product decomposition of the backbone
8814	  push--pull object (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_sigma_iso}), the preservation of products by the
8815	  section functor, and the per-leg section identification
8816	  \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\operatorname{Over.mk} j_{\sigma'})) \cong
8817	  \Gamma(U_{\sigma'} \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_leg_sections}). Projecting
8818	  onto the \(\sigma'\)-leg, the evaluated {\v C}ech-nerve coface \(\delta^{\mathrm{nerve}}_k\)
8819	  acts on sections as the restriction of \(\mathcal{F}\) along the inclusion of the
8820	  \(\sigma'\)-intersection open into the \((\sigma' \circ d_k)\)-intersection open — precisely the
8821	  coface induced by deleting the \(k\)-th index. The open-meet identity
8822	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}) rewrites both intersection opens as the meets
8823	  \(\bigcap_j(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma' j) \cap V)\) and
8824	  \(\bigcap_l(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,((\sigma' \circ d_k) l) \cap V)\), identifying
8825	  this restriction with \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma', k)\) on the nose.
8826	\end{proof}

8828	\begin{lemma}
8829	[Per-coface square of the core comparison]
8830	  \label{lem:coreIso_comm_coface}
8831	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coreIso_comm_coface}
8832	  \uses{lem:coreIso_comm_leg, lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_objd_apply}
8833	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso}, for each degree \(p\) and each coface
8834	  index \(k \le p+1\) the individual cofaces are intertwined by the object isomorphisms:
8835	  \[
8836	    (\mathrm{objIso}\,p).\mathrm{hom} \cdot \delta^{\mathrm{sec}}_k
8837	      \;=\;
8838	    G_V\bigl(\Psi(\delta^{\mathrm{nerve}}_k)\bigr) \cdot (\mathrm{objIso}\,(p+1)).\mathrm{hom},
8839	  \]
8840	  where \(\delta^{\mathrm{sec}}_k\) is the \(k\)-th section-{\v C}ech coface and
8841	  \(\delta^{\mathrm{nerve}}_k\) the \(k\)-th {\v C}ech-nerve coface of the backbone, both maps
8842	  into the degree-\((p+1)\) term.
8843	\end{lemma}
8844	\begin{proof}
8845	  \uses{lem:coreIso_comm_leg, lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_objd_apply}
8846	  Both sides are maps into the degree-\((p+1)\) term, which is the product over multi-indices
8847	  \(\sigma' : \mathrm{Fin}(p+2) \to I\); by the product equivalence
8848	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}) it
8849	  suffices to check equality coordinate by coordinate. Fix \(\sigma'\). The \(\sigma'\)-coordinate
8850	  of the left-hand side is, by the per-coface coordinate computation underlying
8851	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, the presheaf face restriction
8852	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma', k)\) applied to the \((\sigma' \circ d_k)\)-
8853	  coordinate of \((\mathrm{objIso}\,p).\mathrm{hom}\). The \(\sigma'\)-coordinate of the
8854	  right-hand side is the same expression by Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_comm_leg}. Hence all
8855	  coordinates agree and the two cofaces are equal.
8856	\end{proof}

8858	\begin{lemma}
8859	[Alternating-sum assembly of the core comparison square]
8860	  \label{lem:coreIso_comm_sum}
8861	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coreIso_comm_sum}
8862	  \uses{lem:coreIso_comm_coface, lem:section_cech_objd_apply, lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
8863	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso}, for each degree \(p\) the full
8864	  alternating-coface differentials are intertwined by the object isomorphisms:
8865	  \[
8866	    (\mathrm{objIso}\,p).\mathrm{hom} \cdot d^p_{\check{\mathcal{C}}(\mathcal{U}')}
8867	      \;=\;
8868	    d^p_{(G_V\circ\Psi)\check{\mathcal{C}}(\mathcal{U})} \cdot (\mathrm{objIso}\,(p+1)).\mathrm{hom}.
8869	  \]
8870	\end{lemma}
8871	\begin{proof}
8872	  \uses{lem:coreIso_comm_coface, lem:section_cech_objd_apply, lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
8873	  Both differentials are the alternating coface sums \(d^p = \sum_{k=0}^{p+1} (-1)^k
8874	  \delta_k\) (the \(\operatorname{objD}\) of the alternating-coface-map complex). Work
8875	  elementwise: by Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, read through the product equivalence
8876	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), each side — evaluated at an element of the source
8877	  and at a coordinate \(\sigma'\) — is the pointwise finite sum \(\sum_{k} (-1)^k\,(\cdots)_k\) of
8878	  the same shape. The two sums agree summand by summand: the \(k\)-th summands carry the same sign
8879	  \((-1)^k\) and, by the per-coface square of Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_comm_coface}, the same coface
8880	  contribution. A termwise comparison of the two alternating sums therefore yields equality of
8881	  the differentials in every degree.
8882	\end{proof}

8887	[The core comparison intertwines the {\v C}ech differentials]
8888	  \label{lem:coreIso_comm}

8890	  \uses{lem:coreIso_comm_sum, lem:coreIso_obj_iso}
8891	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso}, for each \(p\) the degreewise object
8892	  isomorphisms \(\mathrm{objIso}\,p\) intertwine the two differentials:
8893	  \[

8895	      d^{p}_{\check{\mathcal{C}}(\mathcal{U}')}
8896	      \;=\;
8897	    d^{p}_{(G_V\circ\Psi)\check{\mathcal{C}}(\mathcal{U})} \cdot

8899	  \]

8901	  is an isomorphism of cochain complexes \((G_V\circ\Psi)\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},

8917	  the \(p=0\) object iso \(\mathrm{objIso}\,0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso} at \(p=0\)), is
8918	  the restriction-product map \(\varepsilon\colon t \mapsto (t|_{U'_i})_i\) on the nose, because
8919	  both are induced by the inclusions \(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i \cap V

9095	  isomorphism of cochain complexes \(D^\bullet \cong D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\).
9096	\end{proof}

9101	[The section {\v C}ech complex inside a cover member is contractible]
9102	  \label{lem:cechSection_contractible}
9103	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_contractible}
9104	  % NOTE: build target — augmented form. The contractible complex is the AUGMENTED section
9105	  % complex D'_aug = (sectionCechComplex U'·).augment ε hε, NOT the bare section complex D'
9106	  % (which is not contractible: a one-member cover {V} gives H⁰(D') = Γ(V,F) ≠ 0). The prover
9107	  % re-signs the Lean declaration to Homotopy (𝟙 D'_aug) 0. The dep* engine supplies only the
9108	  % positive-degree homotopy; the augmentation node is a separate explicit degree-0 computation,
9109	  % given below.
9110	  \uses{lem:cechSection_complex_iso, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_engine_complex,
9111	    def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
9112	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
9113	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the projection homotopy
9114	  % $h(s)_{i_0\ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0\ldots i_p}$ contracting the extended
9115	  % {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L83--112).
9116	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
9117	  % at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
9118	  % There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
9119	  % Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. ...
9120	  % Let us define a homotopy
9121	  % $h : \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_{p + 1}}, \mathfrak p}
9122	  % \longrightarrow \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p} M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_p}, \mathfrak p}$
9123	  % by the rule $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}$ ...
9124	  % In other words, $h : \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}, \mathfrak p} \to M_\mathfrak p$
9125	  % is projection onto the factor
9126	  % $M_{f_{i_{\text{fix}}}, \mathfrak p} = M_{\mathfrak p}$ ..."
9127	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
9128	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof, the projection homotopy).}
9129	  Suppose \(V \le \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) for some fixed index
9130	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), i.e.\ \(V\) lies inside one cover member. Then the \emph{augmented}
9131	  concrete section {\v C}ech complex \(D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\) of
9132	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso} --- the section {\v C}ech complex over the family
9133	  \(U'_j = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,j \cap V\), augmented at
9134	  \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) along the restriction product \(\varepsilon\) --- admits a
9135	  contracting homotopy \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}} \simeq 0\).
9136	\end{lemma}
9137	\begin{proof}
9138	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_engine_complex, def:cech_augmentation,
9139	    lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
9140	  Because \(V \le U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\), the restricted family \(U'_j = U_j \cap V\) has a
9141	  \emph{maximum}: \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} = U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap V = V\), and every
9142	  \(U'_j \le V\). Consequently, for each multi-index \(\sigma\),
9143	  \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\,\sigma} = U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap U_\sigma \cap V = U_\sigma \cap
9144	  V = U'_\sigma\): prepending the index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) does not change the open, so the
9145	  prepend map is the \emph{identity} on each coefficient \(\Gamma(U'_\sigma, \mathcal{F})\)
9146	  (the membership criterion \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\) of
9147	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_engine_complex}). The contracting homotopy is the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)
9148	  map, \(h(s)_\sigma = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\,\sigma}\), exactly the projection homotopy of the
9149	  Stacks proof; it splits into two parts.
9150	
9151	  \emph{Positive {\v C}ech degrees \((p \ge 1\)).} On the section {\v C}ech part the
9152	  dependent-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech engine --- \(\operatorname{depDiff}\),
9153	  \(\operatorname{depHomotopy}\), \(\operatorname{depHomotopy\_spec}\),
9154	  \(\operatorname{depDiff\_exact}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} --- supplies the
9155	  prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) homotopy \(h\) with
9156	  \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\), since its unit, shift, and coface-commutation
9157	  compatibilities all hold with the prepend map acting as the identity on each coefficient.
9158	
9159	  \emph{Augmentation node (the piece the engine does not supply).} The engine governs only the
9160	  {\v C}ech degrees; the contracting-homotopy identity at the augmentation node must be checked
9161	  directly. The relevant homotopy component runs from the lowest {\v C}ech term
9162	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) down to the augmentation term
9163	  \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\), and is the \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)-th coordinate projection
9164	  \(\pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} : (s_j)_j \mapsto s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\) --- well defined because
9165	  \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} = V\), so \(s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \in \Gamma(U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap V,
9166	  \mathcal{F}) = \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\). We verify
9167	  \(\varepsilon \circ \pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} + (\text{degree-}0\ \text{engine term}) = \mathrm{id}\)
9168	  on \(s \in \prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})\). The augmentation differential
9169	  \(\varepsilon\) is the restriction product, so
9170	  \(\bigl(\varepsilon(\pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} s)\bigr)_j = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}|_{U_j \cap V}\); the
9171	  degree-\(0\) contribution of the {\v C}ech homotopy supplies the complementary term
9172	  \(s_j - s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}|_{U_j \cap V}\) coming from the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) shift on
9173	  the {\v C}ech part. Their sum is \(s_j\), i.e.\ the identity. (This is the bottom node of the
9174	  Stacks projection homotopy: \(h\) on \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to M\) is projection onto the
9175	  factor \(M_{f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}} = M\).) Together the two parts give a homotopy
9176	  \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}} \simeq 0\) of cochain complexes.
9177	\end{proof}
9178	
9179	\begin{lemma}
9180	[Local vanishing of the evaluated augmented {\v C}ech homology]
9181	  \label{lem:cechSection_isZero_homology}
9182	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_isZero_homology}
9183	  \uses{lem:cechSection_complex_iso, lem:cechSection_contractible,
9184	    lem:isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero}
9185	  Let \(V \le \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i\) for some index \(i\). Then for every
9186	  degree \(p\) the homology \(H^p(D^\bullet)\) of the augmented {\v C}ech complex evaluated at
9187	  \(V\) (the complex \(D^\bullet\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso}) is a zero
9188	  object; equivalently \(D^\bullet\) carries a contracting homotopy \(\mathrm{id}_{D^\bullet}
9189	  \simeq 0\). This is the residual obligation of Step~3 of
Now let me examine the key infrastructure in CechAcyclic.lean and PresheafCech.lean that the chain must use.

40	`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`. -/
41	
42	namespace CombinatorialCech
43	
44	variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}
45	
46	/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
47	`Cⁿ = (Fin n → ι) → M ⟶ Cⁿ⁺¹`, `(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`.
48	The argument `σ ∘ j.succAbove` is the `(n)`-tuple obtained from the `(n+1)`-tuple
49	`σ` by deleting the `j`-th index. -/
50	private def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
51	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
52	
53	/-- The contracting homotopy of the localised complex: prepend the fixed
54	(globally invertible after localising at `s_r`) index `r`.
55	`(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`. -/
56	private def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
57	  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)
58	
59	@[simp] private lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
60	    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
61	  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]
62	
63	/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(j+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the
64	`j`-th coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
65	lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
66	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
67	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
68	  funext l
69	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
70	  · simp
71	  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]
72	
73	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3; Stacks
74	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`):
75	`d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on `Cⁿ⁺¹`.  This is the alternating-sum cancellation: the
76	`j = 0` term of `h (d t)` is `t`, and the remaining terms cancel against `d (h t)`
77	in pairs of opposite sign. -/
78	private lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
79	    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
80	  funext σ
81	  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
82	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
83	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
84	  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
85	    funext i; simp
86	  rw [h0]
87	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
88	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
89	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
90	    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]
91	
92	/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then
93	`t = d (h t)`.  This is the homological content of `combHomotopy_spec` and is the
94	half (`ker d ⊆ im d`) carrying the geometric input. -/
95	private lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
96	    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
97	  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
98	  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
99	  exact h
100	
101	/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
102	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
103	private lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
104	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
105	      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
106	            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
107	  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
108	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
109	        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
110	    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
111	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
112	    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
113	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
114	        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
115	    rw [pow_succ]; ring
116	
117	/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex.  Proved by
118	the standard sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` on
119	the double sum: the swap fixes the underlying composite coface
120	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`), is an involution
121	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`, `Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove`), has no
122	fixed point (`Fin.succAbove_ne`), and flips the sign (`combSign_flip`). -/
123	private lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
124	    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
125	  funext σ
126	  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
127	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
128	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
129	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
130	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
131	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
132	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
133	      funext k
134	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
135	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
136	    simp only [harg]
137	    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
138	    simp
139	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
140	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
141	    intro hj
142	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
143	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
144	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
145	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
146	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
147	
148	/-- **Positive-degree exactness** of the constant-coefficient Čech complex in the
149	`Function.Exact` form that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner L2) consumes node
150	by node.  Combines `combDifferential_comp` (`im ⊆ ker`) with
151	`combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle` (`ker ⊆ im`, the homotopy half).  Requires a
152	distinguished index `r : ι` — supplied, after localising at `s_r`, by the
153	spanning element itself. -/
154	private lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
155	    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
156	      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
157	  intro x
158	  constructor
159	  · intro hx
160	    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
161	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
162	    exact combDifferential_comp y
163	
164	/-! ### Dependent-coefficient port (planner **L3 port**)
165	
166	The localised complex that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner **L2**) consumes
167	node-by-node has *varying* coefficients `M_{s_σ}` — the away localisation of `M`
168	at the product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}` — not a single constant module `M`.  After
169	localising the whole extended complex at a spanning element `s_r`, the index `r`
170	is globally available and `s_r` becomes a unit, so the prepend
171	`σ ↦ Fin.cons r σ` is an *isomorphism* on the coefficients:
172	`M_{s_{cons r σ}} = M_{s_r · s_σ} ≅ M_{s_σ}` because `s_r` is already invertible.
173	
174	This section ports the constant-coefficient cancellation
175	(`combHomotopy_spec`, `combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle`) to that dependent
176	setting.  The away-localisation restriction maps are abstracted as additive
177	coface maps `δ` (`M_{s_{σ∘dⱼ}} → M_{s_σ}`, well defined because `s_σ` carries the
178	extra factor `s_{σ j}`) and the prepend isomorphisms as additive maps `c`
179	(`M_{s_{cons r σ}} → M_{s_σ}`).  The two compatibility identities below are
180	exactly the ones the away-localisation maps satisfy:
181	
182	* the **unit** identity `c ∘ δ₀ = id` (deleting the prepended `r` from `cons r σ`
183	  recovers `σ`; on localisations the composite restriction is the identity since
184	  `s_r` is a unit), and
185	* the **shift** identity `c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c` (prepend commutes with the later
186	  cofaces), which is `cons_comp_succAbove_succ` lifted to the localisation maps.
187	
188	Threading the dependent family through the same alternating-sum cancellation as
189	the constant case then yields the dependent homotopy identity and the
190	cocycle⟹coboundary corollary that L2 consumes.  Constructing the actual `δ`/`c`
191	from `IsLocalizedModule.Away` (Mathlib `tilde` API:
192	`AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde`, `IsLocalizedModule (.powers f)`) and the L1
193	identification of the abstract `CechComplex` terms with `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` remain the
194	outstanding bridge (see `CechAcyclic.affine`). -/
195	
196	section Dependent
197	
198	variable {A : (m : ℕ) → (Fin m → ι) → Type*} [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)]
199	variable (r : ι)
200	variable (δ : (m : ℕ) → (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) → (j : Fin (m + 1)) →
201	    A m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →+ A (m + 1) σ)
202	variable (c : (m : ℕ) → (σ : Fin m → ι) → A (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) →+ A m σ)
203	
204	omit [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)] in
205	/-- Transport of a dependent cochain value along an equality of index tuples.
206	The transport friction that the constant-coefficient proof avoided (there every
207	coefficient is the same `M`) is isolated here. -/
208	lemma depTransport {m : ℕ} {x y : Fin m → ι} (h : x = y)
209	    (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) : h ▸ t x = t y := by
210	  subst h; rfl
211	
212	/-- Deleting the prepended index `r` (the `0`-th coface of `Fin.cons r σ`)
213	recovers `σ`. -/
214	lemma cons_comp_zero_succAbove {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) :
215	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 1)).succAbove = σ := by
216	  funext i; simp
217	
218	/-- Dependent (varying-coefficient) alternating Čech differential built from the
219	coface (localisation restriction) maps `δ`. -/
220	def depDiff {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
221	    ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ :=
222	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • δ m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove))
223	
224	/-- Dependent contracting homotopy: prepend the distinguished index `r`, then
225	apply the prepend map `c`. -/
226	def depHomotopy {m : ℕ} (u : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) :
227	    ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ :=
228	  fun σ => c m σ (u (Fin.cons r σ))
229	
230	/-- **Dependent contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3 port): `d ∘ h + h ∘ d
231	= id`, evaluated at a tuple `σ`.  Same alternating-sum cancellation as
232	`combHomotopy_spec`, threaded through the varying coefficients via the unit
233	identity `hu` (`c ∘ δ₀ = id`) and the shift identity `hsh`
234	(`c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c`). -/
235	lemma depHomotopy_spec
236	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
237	        (y : A (m + 1)
238	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
239	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
240	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
241	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
242	        (y : A (m + 1)
243	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
244	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
245	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
246	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
247	    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ + depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = t σ := by
248	  simp only [depDiff, depHomotopy]
249	  rw [map_sum]
250	  simp only [map_zsmul]
251	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun k : Fin (m + 2) =>
252	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (k : ℕ) • c (m + 1) σ
253	      (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) k (t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ k.succAbove))))]
254	  -- the `k = 0` term collapses to `t σ` via the unit identity
255	  rw [hu σ (t _)]
256	  rw [depTransport (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) t]
257	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
258	  -- the `k = j.succ` terms cancel against the `d ∘ h` sum in pairs of opposite sign
259	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
260	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
261	    rw [hsh σ x (t _), depTransport (cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ x) t,
262	      pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]
263	    abel), add_zero]
264	
265	/-- Dependent cocycle⟹coboundary (the geometric half consumed by L2): if
266	`depDiff t = 0` then `t = depDiff (depHomotopy t)`. -/
267	lemma depDiff_eq_of_cocycle
268	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
269	        (y : A (m + 1)
270	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
271	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
272	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
273	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
274	        (y : A (m + 1)
275	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
276	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
277	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
278	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ)
279	    (ht : depDiff δ t = 0) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
280	    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ = t σ := by
281	  have h := depHomotopy_spec r δ c hu hsh t σ
282	  rw [show depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = 0 by rw [ht]; simp [depHomotopy], add_zero] at h
283	  exact h
284	
285	omit [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)] in
286	/-- The composite coface tuple is symmetric under the `d²=0` index swap
287	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` (dependent analogue of the `harg` step
288	inside `combDifferential_comp`). -/
289	lemma comp_succAbove_swap {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι)
290	    (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1)) :
291	    (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
292	      = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
293	  funext k
294	  simp only [Function.comp_apply]
295	  rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
296	
297	/-- **Dependent `d² = 0`** for the varying-coefficient alternating Čech complex.
298	Same sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` as
299	`combDifferential_comp`, with the coefficient transport handled by
300	`comp_succAbove_swap` and the coface-commutation hypothesis `hcomm` (the two
301	restriction maps into `M_{s_σ}` agree — a formal property of localisation maps,
302	independent of the choice of `r`). -/
303	lemma depDiff_comp
304	    (hcomm : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
305	        (z : A m ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)),
306	        δ (m + 1) σ j (δ m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
307	          = δ (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
308	              (δ m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
309	                ((comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)))
310	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
311	    depDiff δ (depDiff δ t) = 0 := by
312	  funext σ
313	  simp only [depDiff, Pi.zero_apply, map_sum, map_zsmul, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
314	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (m + 2) × Fin (m + 1) =>
315	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) •
316	      δ (m + 1) σ p.1 (δ m (σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) p.2 (t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))))]
317	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
318	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
319	    simp only []

1470	    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op).hom
1471	      = AwayComparison.comparison (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1472	          (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb := by
1473	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
1474	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom := inferInstance
1475	  refine (AwayComparison.comparison_unique
1476	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1477	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb _ ?_).symm
1478	  have h := AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen_res M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1479	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b) i
1480	  exact congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom h

1482	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech homology bridge (L1 steps c, d)
1483	
1484	The categorical→module bridge of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact`: the
1485	`Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (of `PresheafCech.lean`)
1486	has its degree-`p` object a *categorical product* `∏ᶜ_σ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` in `Ab`, and
1487	its differential the alternating sum of the {\v C}ech coface restrictions.  These
1488	lemmas (c1)–(c3) move that abstract complex to the concrete localised-module complex
1489	`SectionCechModule.dDiff` (whose positive-degree exactness `dDiff_exact` is step (a)),
1490	and read off homology vanishing. -/
1491	
1492	section SectionCechBridge
1493	
1494	open CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicTopology
1495	
1496	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1497	
1498	/-- **(c1) Element-level product equivalence** (`lem:section_cech_product_equiv`):
1499	the underlying type of the degree-`p` object `∏ᶜ_σ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of the section
1500	{\v C}ech cosimplicial object is the dependent product `∏_σ ToType (F(⨅ₖ U (σ k)))`.
1501	Supplied by `CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv` (the forgetful functor of
1502	`Ab` preserves discrete products). -/
1503	noncomputable def sectionCechProductEquiv {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1504	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) :
1505	    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≃
1506	      (∀ σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι, ToType (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) :=
1507	  Concrete.productEquiv
1508	    (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
1509	
1510	/-- Coordinate projection of `sectionCechProductEquiv`: the `σ`-component is the
1511	underlying group map of the categorical projection `Pi.π … σ`.  A definitional
1512	restatement of `Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply`, named so that downstream proofs can
1513	`rw` it without fighting the `Concrete.productEquiv` coercion. -/
1514	lemma sectionCechProductEquiv_apply {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1515	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ)
1516	    (y : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
1517	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
1518	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F p y σ
1519	      = ConcreteCategory.hom
1520	          (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ) y :=
1521	  Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply _ y σ
1522	
1523	/-- **Homology-to-exactness reduction** (the `Ab`-side of `lem:section_cech_ab_exact`):
1524	the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the section {\v C}ech complex vanishes once the
1525	underlying group homomorphisms of the two consecutive coface differentials
1526	`objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence.  Pure homological algebra: combines
1527	`exactAt_iff_isZero_homology`, `exactAt_iff'`, and the abelian-group exactness
1528	criterion `ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact`. -/
1529	lemma sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact {ι : Type u}
1530	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
1531	    (h : Function.Exact
1532	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
1533	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
1534	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1)))) :
1535	    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1)) := by
1536	  rw [← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
1537	      (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
1538	      ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact]
1539	  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
1540	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
1541	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) q
1542	  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
1543	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
1544	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) (q + 1)
1545	  change Function.Exact
1546	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
1547	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)))
1548	  rw [hf, hg]
1549	  exact h
1550	
1551	/-- Application of a finite sum of `Ab`-morphisms distributes over the sum. -/
1552	private lemma ab_hom_finsetSum_apply {A B : Ab.{u}} {κ : Type*}
1553	    (s : Finset κ) (f : κ → (A ⟶ B)) (t : ToType A) :
1554	    ConcreteCategory.hom (∑ i ∈ s, f i) t = ∑ i ∈ s, ConcreteCategory.hom (f i) t := by
1555	  classical
1556	  induction s using Finset.induction with
1557	  | empty => simp
1558	  | insert a s ha ih =>
1559	      rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, AddCommGrpCat.hom_add_apply, ih]
1560	
1561	/-- The `i`-th section {\v C}ech face restriction at a multi-index `σ` of the
1562	cosimplicial section object: the presheaf restriction of `F` from the deleted-face
1563	intersection `⨅ₗ U (σ (δ i l))` to the full intersection `⨅ₖ U (σ k)`.  Factored as a
1564	named `def` so the cosimplicial differential (`sectionCech_objD_apply`) refers to it
1565	by name rather than re-elaborating the `homOfLE (le_iInf …)` term. -/
1566	noncomputable def sectionCechFaceRestr {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1567	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
1568	    F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ l, U ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
1569	      ⟶ F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))) :=
1570	  F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf
1571	    (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op
1572	
1573	/-- **(c2, abstract) Coface match**: the underlying group action of the section
1574	{\v C}ech coface differential `objD q`, read through the product equivalence, is the
1575	alternating sum of the presheaf restriction maps applied to the deleted-face
1576	coordinates — exactly the shape of `SectionCechModule.dDiff`.  This is purely about
1577	the cosimplicial structure of `sectionCechCosimplicial`; no sheaf identification yet. -/
1578	lemma sectionCech_objD_apply {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1579	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
1580	    (t : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk q)))
1581	    (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) :
1582	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F (q + 1)
1583	        (ConcreteCategory.hom
1584	          (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t) σ
1585	      = ∑ i : Fin (q + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
1586	          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
1587	            (sectionCechProductEquiv U F q t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
1588	  classical
1589	  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply,
1590	      show ConcreteCategory.hom
1591	            (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ)
1592	            (ConcreteCategory.hom
1593	              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t)
1594	          = ConcreteCategory.hom
1595	              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q
1596	                ≫ Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ) t
1597	          from rfl,
1598	      AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, Preadditive.sum_comp, ab_hom_finsetSum_apply]
1599	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
1600	  rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]
1601	  simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_zsmul, AddMonoidHom.smul_apply]
1602	  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]
1603	  congr 1
1604	  have hmap : (sectionCechCosimplicial U F).map (SimplexCategory.δ i) ≫ Pi.π _ σ
1605	      = Pi.π _ (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫ sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i :=
1606	    Pi.lift_π _ σ
1607	  rw [CosimplicialObject.δ, hmap]
1608	  rfl
1609	
1610	end SectionCechBridge
1611	
1612	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — tilde-bridge and affine vanishing (L1 steps A, B, d)
1613	
1614	The final stretch of the L1 categorical→module bridge for the **tilde sheaf**
1615	`F = ~M`.  The previous section moved the abstract `Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex
1616	to the shape of an alternating sum of presheaf restrictions
1617	(`sectionCech_objD_apply`); here we identify, degreewise, those section groups with the
1618	away-localisation coefficients `dCoeff` and the restrictions with the localisation
1619	cofaces `dCoface`, via the per-σ comparison `φ_σ` (`IsLocalizedModule.iso`).  Transporting
1620	the already-proven module exactness `SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact` across the resulting
1621	degreewise additive isomorphism (`Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact`) discharges
1622	the underlying-group exactness, which `sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact` turns
1623	into positive-degree homology vanishing of the section {\v C}ech complex
1624	(`sectionCech_affine_vanishing`). -/
1625	
1626	section SectionCechTilde
1627	
1628	open AlgebraicTopology Scheme.Modules SectionCechModule CechLocalized AwayComparison
1629	
1630	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {ι : Type u} (s : ι → R)
1631	
1632	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1633	-- raised: the `IsLocalizedModule.ext` reduction over the heavy `modulesSpecToSheaf` section
1634	-- types is defeq-intensive (the accessor-2 `~M` sections do not reduce cheaply).
1635	/-- **Linear-level per-coface naturality** (`R`-linear).  The comparison `φ_σ`
1636	(written here as the raw `IsLocalizedModule.iso ▸ symm`, definitionally `phiL` below)
1637	intertwines the (accessor-2) {\v C}ech face restriction with the away-localisation coface
1638	`dCoface`.  Proved by `IsLocalizedModule.ext`: both composites send the localisation unit
1639	`toOpen M (D s_{σ∘dᵢ})` of `M` to the localisation unit `mkLinearMap (powers s_σ)`, by
1640	`tilde.toOpen_res` (restriction commutes with `toOpen`), `iso_symm_comp`, and
1641	`comparison_apply` (the defining property of `dCoface`).  Stated and proved *before* the
1642	`phiL` abbreviation so its `IsLocalizedModule.ext` elaboration is not slowed by
1643	fold/unfold attempts against `phiL` — keeping it within the heartbeat budget. -/
1644	private lemma phiL_naturality {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
1645	    haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
1646	    (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1647	        (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ
1648	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
1649	          (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _
1650	            ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom
1651	      = (dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i) ∘ₗ
1652	        (haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
1653	         (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
1654	          (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k,
1655	            PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom).symm.toLinearMap) := by
1656	  haveI i1 := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
1657	  haveI i2 := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
1658	  -- Abstract the concrete face-restriction map as an opaque `g` BEFORE `IsLocalizedModule.ext`,
1659	  -- so `ext` never whnf's the (heartbeat-heavy) `modulesSpecToSheaf` restriction.  `hg` records
1660	  -- its `toOpen` compatibility (`tilde.toOpen_res`).
1661	  set g := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
1662	      (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _
1663	        ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom with hg_def
1664	  have hg : ∀ m : M,
1665	      g ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom m)
1666	        = (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom m := by
1667	    intro m
1668	    rw [hg_def]
1669	    exact congrFun (congrArg (fun l => (l.hom : _ → _))

100	relative {\v C}ech complex. Project-local: packages the cover as a single arrow
101	so the existing `Arrow.augmentedCechNerve` machinery applies. -/
102	noncomputable def coverArrow (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : Arrow Scheme.{u} :=
103	  Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)
104	
105	/-- The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an open cover `𝒰`, as an augmented
106	simplicial scheme over `X`: in simplicial degree `p` it is the `(p+1)`-fold
107	fibre power of `∐ᵢ Uᵢ` over `X`, i.e. `∐_{(i₀,…,i_p)} U_{i₀} ×ₓ ⋯ ×ₓ U_{i_p}`,
108	with augmentation the cover map to `X`. Exists unconditionally because `Scheme`
109	has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks used by
110	`Arrow.augmentedCechNerve`). Project-local geometric backbone for `CechNerve`. -/
111	noncomputable def coverCechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
112	    SimplicialObject.Augmented Scheme.{u} :=
113	  (coverArrow 𝒰).augmentedCechNerve
114	
115	/-! ### Push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules` — object and morphism bricks
116	
117	The geometric backbone above is lifted to a cosimplicial `O_X`-module by the
118	*relative-direct-image functor on the over-category*
119	```
120	  G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules,   (Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F,
121	```
122	sending an `X`-scheme `p : Y ⟶ X` to the pushforward along `p` of the pullback
123	`p^* F`. We record here the two *pre-coherence* bricks of `G` — its action on
124	objects (`pushPullObj`, the planner's `Gobj`) and on morphisms (`pushPullMap`,
125	the planner's `Gmap`) — both axiom-clean and free of any functor law. The functor
126	laws `G(𝟙) = 𝟙` and `G(g ≫ h) = G(h) ≫ G(g)` are a *consumer* of the
127	pushforward/pullback composition coherence (`pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` and
128	their unitor/pentagon identities) and are deferred; see the note after
129	`pushPullMap`. -/
130	
131	/-- The object map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`,
132	`(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. Sends an `X`-scheme `Y` (with structure map `Y.hom : Y.left
133	⟶ X`) to the pushforward along `Y.hom` of the pullback of `F`. Project-local
134	object brick of the {\v C}ech push–pull functor (the planner's `Gobj`). -/
135	noncomputable def pushPullObj (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) : X.Modules :=
136	  (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
137	
138	/-- The morphism map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. For a
139	morphism `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁` of `X`-schemes (so `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`, the
140	over-triangle `Over.w g`), the contravariant functor produces the restriction
141	comparison `pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂`. It is the three-step composite:
142	the unit `η` of the adjunction `pullback g.left ⊣ pushforward g.left`, the
143	pushforward comparison `(pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, and the pushforward
144	of the pullback comparison `(pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, glued by two
145	`eqToHom` transports along the over-triangle `Over.w g`. No functor law is used:
146	this is a reusable pre-coherence brick (the planner's `Gmap`). -/
147	noncomputable def pushPullMap (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
148	    pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂ :=
149	  (pushforward Y₁.hom).map
150	      ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g.left).unit.app
151	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
152	    (pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app
153	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
154	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward q).obj
155	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)))
156	      (Over.w g)) ≫
157	    (pushforward Y₂.hom).map ((pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app F) ≫
158	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward Y₂.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F))
159	      (Over.w g))
160	
161	/- **The functor laws `pushPullMap_id` / `pushPullMap_comp`.**
162	Assembling `pushPullObj` / `pushPullMap` into the functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤
163	X.Modules` requires
164	```

547	/-- The geometric Čech backbone, lifted to a **simplicial object in `Over X`**: each
548	fibre power carries its structure map to `X` (the augmentation of `coverCechNerve`),
549	and the face/degeneracy maps are `X`-morphisms by naturality of the augmentation into
550	the constant functor. Project-local; obtained from `coverCechNerve` by
551	`CategoryTheory.Over.lift` applied to the augmentation natural transformation. -/
552	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOver (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : SimplicialObject (Over X) :=
553	  Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom
554	
555	/-- The over-category Čech backbone as an **augmented** simplicial object in `Over X`:
556	`coverCechNerveOver` augmented by the terminal object `Over.mk (𝟙 X)` of `Over X`. The
557	augmentation map at each simplicial level is the unique morphism to the terminal object
558	(its underlying scheme map is the level's structure map to `X`), and the augmentation
559	coherence condition holds automatically because the augmentation target is terminal.
560	Project-local. -/
561	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOverAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
562	    SimplicialObject.Augmented (Over X) :=
563	  SimplicialObject.augment (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰) (Over.mk (𝟙 X))
564	    (Over.mkIdTerminal.from _)
565	    (fun _ _ _ => Over.mkIdTerminal.hom_ext _ _)
566	
567	/-- The cosimplicial `O_X`-module obtained by post-composing the over-category Čech
568	backbone `coverCechNerveOver` (read contravariantly, via `Functor.leftOp`) with the
569	push–pull functor `pushPullFunctor F = G`. This is the underlying cosimplicial object
570	of `CechNerve` (before adjoining the augmentation `F`). Project-local. -/
571	noncomputable def cechNerveCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
572	    CosimplicialObject X.Modules :=
573	  (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰 : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X).rightOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F

575	/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
576	cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).
577	
578	For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
579	sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules whose
580	object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the `(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of
581	indices, of the direct images `(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of
582	`F` to the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
583	immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that omit one
584	index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree `-1` is `F` itself
585	on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection `U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.
586	
587	This is now *constructed*, not postulated: the geometric backbone `coverCechNerveOverAug`
588	(the augmented Čech nerve of the cover as an augmented simplicial object in `Over X`,
589	unconditional because `Scheme` has all finite limits) is read contravariantly
590	(`SimplicialObject.Augmented.rightOp`) and whiskered by the push–pull functor
591	`pushPullFunctor F = G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. The whiskering
592	transports both the cosimplicial structure and the augmentation, so the augmentation
593	point is `G` applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`, namely
594	`(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`. The functor laws of `G` (`pushPullMap_id`, `pushPullMap_comp`)
595	are what make this assembly legitimate.
596	
597	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
598	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
599	noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
600	    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
601	  (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)).obj
602	    (coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).rightOp
603	
604	/-- **Relative {\v C}ech complex from a cosimplicial nerve** (coherence-free
605	plumbing). Given `f : X ⟶ S` and an augmented cosimplicial object `N` of
606	`O_X`-modules, produce the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` by:
607	forgetting the augmentation (`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop`), pushing the
608	cosimplicial object forward along `f` (`CosimplicialObject.whiskering` applied to
609	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`), and taking the alternating coface-map cochain
610	complex (`alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). This is the entire passage `CechNerve ↦
611	CechComplex`, and it uses no `pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence — only
612	the (pre)additivity of `S.Modules`. Project-local. -/
613	noncomputable def relativeCechComplexOfNerve (f : X ⟶ S)
614	    (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules) : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
615	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S.Modules).obj
616	    (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
617	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N))
618	
619	/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
620	Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).
621	
622	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
623	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
624	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
625	```
626	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
627	```
628	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
629	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
630	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
631	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
632	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
633	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
634	quasi-coherent.
635	
636	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
637	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
638	noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
639	    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
640	  -- Construction (Stacks): apply the relative pushforward `f_*` over each finite
641	  -- intersection to the Čech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, then take the alternating-sum
642	  -- Čech differential. This is exactly the coherence-free plumbing
643	  -- `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, so `CechComplex` is genuinely *defined* in terms
644	  -- of the nerve: an axiom-clean `CechNerve` immediately yields an axiom-clean
645	  -- `CechComplex`. The only remaining hole is `CechNerve` itself.
646	  relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)
